scope.ac_basis: close.md AC-2.6 cleanliness-of-THIS-diff scoping; line-level default-safe rule; spec-20260503-091826 §5.4 rule 3 (pre-existing patterns preserved on modified lines = pre-existing)
scope.pre_diff_baseline_method: git diff HEAD -- <path> for each of the 25 files; baseline = HEAD before this cycle's uncommitted modifications

standards_evaluated.name: no-inline-code
standards_evaluated.rationale: Only newly-added code block is in agents/qa.md (Verification harness cleanup contract) which is explicitly an EXAMPLE pattern with `<spawn-command>` placeholder; not inline operational code. commands/commit.md Step 7 algorithm is structured prose with regex strings, not pseudocode/inline-exec. commands/push.md added an `exec` example line within an existing ```bash block (illustrative, with `${RESOLVED_REMOTE}` placeholder).

standards_evaluated.name: no-hardcoded-values
standards_evaluated.verdict: no critical violations; one ADVISORY-coupled with Standard 9 (see advisory_findings)
standards_evaluated.rationale: hooks/lib/allowlist.py introduces SENTINEL_GRANT_DIR as a module-level named constant (good). Legacy /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{sid}.json literals are CONTRACT literals (production module + hook + tests must agree); the existing-cycle pattern of accepting these as test-fixture coupling is preserved. CLAUDE.md /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json reference is documentation describing the contract.

standards_evaluated.name: use-source-venv
standards_evaluated.applicability: shell .sh and command .md that invoke python
standards_evaluated.rationale: Four new bare python3 invocations introduced in hooks/push.sh:18,20,43, hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:186, hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:41. Pre-existing baseline of these hook files already had bare python3 widely (push.sh baseline: 5+ python3 sites at lines 86,88,92,248,253). Verifier scripts (_ac*_verify.sh, _final_sweep.sh, test_push_sentinel_abort.sh) use bare python3 by spec mandate per user-provided context note. pretool-bash-safety.sh new sentinel-gate calls properly use $PYTHON_BIN (parameterized) — correctly addresses the standard. Per AC-2.6 default-safe rule + file-convention-consistency, classified ADVISORY rather than CRITICAL.

standards_evaluated.name: integer-step-numbering
standards_evaluated.rationale: No decimal (1.1) or lettered (1a) step numbering introduced. commands/commit.md Step 7 uses parenthesized integers (1), (2), (3), (4) as sub-algorithm markers within Step 7 — these are integer-sequence and within a single top-level step, NOT a Standard-4 violation pattern.

standards_evaluated.name: meaningful-naming
standards_evaluated.rationale: All newly-added identifiers are verb-phrase or noun-phrase descriptive. None matches forbidden patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, optimize-*, improved-*, better-*, new-*). PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS is an explicit named-constant fix for AC4 (per user context). 'new-commits range' in policy rationale at line 1531 is the noun phrase 'new commits' — false-positive on naive grep, NOT a script prefix.

standards_evaluated.name: english-only
standards_evaluated.applicability: all source files; unconditional full scan of agents/+commands/ even in --changed-files mode
standards_evaluated.verdict: ONE CRITICAL VIOLATION introduced in diff (see violations[])
standards_evaluated.rationale: Unconditional Standard-6 scan of all agents/*.md + commands/*.md + .claude/agents/*.md + .claude/commands/*.md ran. Pre-existing Chinese-character occurrences in commands/dev.md (lines 253, 357, 609, 660, 800, 1480, 1482), commands/close.md (lines 377-391), hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (line 832), hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (line 45) are ALL pre-existing (no +-prefix in this cycle's diff for those lines). Only ONE newly-introduced Chinese-character line in this cycle: commands/commit.md:130 — see violations[] entry.

standards_evaluated.name: merge-similar-scripts
standards_evaluated.rationale: No new files created in scripts/ this cycle. Function additions inside existing files (hooks/lib/allowlist.py extended with sentinel-grant lifecycle; hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py extended with sentinel-consume logic) consolidate functionality into existing modules — the OPPOSITE of fragmentation. test_allowlist_consolidation.py extends an existing test module rather than spawning a new one.

standards_evaluated.name: concise-documentation
standards_evaluated.rationale: Newly-added content is content-justified and structured: commands/commit.md Step 7 algorithm is a deterministic total-ordered contract (necessary for AC5-V3 verification); commands/close.md / commands/dev.md TodoWrite ordering reminders are 2-3 lines each (concise); agents/qa.md Verification harness cleanup contract is one canonical bash pattern + four DO NOT clauses (no repetitive examples); agents/changelog-analyst.md Reversal-citation rule is a single binding-landing clause with two distinct rule disambiguation paragraphs. No marketing language ('amazing', 'fast', 'optimized'), no story-style text, no 3+ repetitive examples.

standards_evaluated.name: parameterized-scripts
standards_evaluated.rationale: agents/push-analyst.md introduces PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 as a named constant replacing the prior inline `timedelta(seconds=120)` — this is the AC4 fix and is correct. hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:192 has an inline `ttl = 300` magic number inside the python3 heredoc — advisory recommendation to similarly extract as named constant. Other newly-added Python constants (CF-3 binding fields, classify_terminal_result return labels) use enum-style string constants which are appropriate for that domain.

standards_evaluated.name: dangling-references
standards_evaluated.rationale: Only one newly-added script reference: commands/push.md line ~218 mentions `scripts/write-commit-grant.py` — verified to exist on disk. agents/push-analyst.md cross-references to its own Phase 7 section (intra-file, not dangling). hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py:104 imports `_enumerate_sentinel_grant_files` from `lib.allowlist` — module-internal import, resolves correctly.

standards_evaluated.name: no-merge-conflict-markers
standards_evaluated.rationale: Grepped all 25 changed files for `^(<<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>) ` conflict markers — 0 hits.

violations.finding: Newly-added line contains verbatim Chinese user-binding directive in backticks: `禁止加载任何非本cycle的内容` (English: 'forbidden to load any non-current-cycle content'). Standard 6 exemption explicitly scopes verbatim non-English user-binding quotes to `docs/dev/ticket-*.md` ONLY. A command/.md file is NOT a ticket file. The exemption clause reads: 'Verbatim non-English user-binding quotes belong in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only ... Code/script comments and user-visible diagnostic strings ... must be English with task-id attribution citing the ticket where the verbatim text is preserved.'
violations.mitigating_factors: Phrase is wrapped in backticks as explicit verbatim citation. English translation provided in same paragraph ('Prior-cycle artifacts MUST NOT be matched...'). Task-id attribution implicit via 'AC5-V3' spec reference. Removing the literal Chinese would lose the binding directive's literal force.
violations.recommendation: Two acceptable resolutions: (a) Move the verbatim Chinese citation to docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md (the ticket already exists per dev cycle artifacts), and replace the inline backticked Chinese at commit.md:130 with an English paraphrase plus a citation reference: 'This operationalizes the user binding directive (task 20260519-211515 §verbatim-bindings; preserved verbatim in docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md).' (b) Keep the Chinese citation but add an explicit task-id + ticket-file attribution citation in the same paragraph so the Standard-6 exemption's 'task-id attribution citing the ticket where the verbatim text is preserved' clause is plainly satisfied: append 'Verbatim Chinese preserved per docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md §user-bindings; English translation provided above.' Either resolution satisfies Standard 6.
violations.disposition_rationale: Inspector reports; does not auto-fix. Cleanliness-of-this-diff CRITICAL per close.md AC-2.6 because the line is provably NEW (absent from pre-diff baseline) and falls under Standard 6's exemption-narrow-scope rule. Caller (close.md) determines whether to block-on-critical, accept-with-fix-deferred, or hot-fix this cycle.

advisory_findings.finding: Four newly-added inline `python3 -c` (or `python3 - <<'PYEOF'`) invocations in hook scripts. Standard 3 literal reading: 'All Python script invocations MUST use source venv'. These new sites add to a file-pre-existing convention — baseline hooks/push.sh already had bare python3 at lines 86, 88, 92, 248, 253. hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh's new sentinel-gate code correctly uses parameterized `$PYTHON_BIN` (the correct pattern). Verifier scripts (_ac*_verify.sh, _final_sweep.sh, test_push_sentinel_abort.sh) use bare python3 by spec-mandated AC-verification heredoc convention (per user-provided context note).
advisory_findings.recommendation: Optional: introduce a single PYTHON_BIN variable at the top of push.sh / userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh / stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh (matching the parameterization pattern already in pretool-bash-safety.sh) and replace bare `python3` calls. Not required for close-cleanliness since the file-convention-consistency rule preserves the pattern.
advisory_findings.disposition_rationale: ADVISORY because (a) file-pre-existing baseline already established the bare-python3 convention extensively, (b) pretool-bash-safety.sh new sites DO use the parameterized $PYTHON_BIN form (showing the standard IS being honored where introduced fresh), (c) hook bootstrap scripts often run before venv is in scope. Per AC-2.6 default-safe rule, classified ADVISORY rather than CRITICAL.

advisory_findings.finding: Inline `ttl = 300` magic number inside the new sentinel-grant write heredoc. The 300s TTL is the sentinel-grant validity window; it parallels the AC4 fix that introduced PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 as a named constant for push-analyst grants. The userprompt sentinel TTL did not receive the same named-constant treatment.
advisory_findings.recommendation: Optional refactor: extract as named constant SENTINEL_TTL_SECONDS = 300 (or DEFAULT_SENTINEL_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 300) at the top of the heredoc or in hooks/lib/allowlist.py for symmetry with the AC4 PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS pattern. Not required for close-cleanliness; the comment on the same line ('Default sentinel TTL: 300s. Aligns with bash-safety grant window.') documents the value, reducing the magic-number harm.
advisory_findings.disposition_rationale: ADVISORY because (a) the value is documented by inline comment, (b) the cycle's primary AC4 named-constant fix targets push-analyst specifically (not this code path), (c) the existing test fixtures don't bind on this value. Refactoring symmetric to PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS is a clean follow-up but not a close-blocker.

observations.standard: module-private import boundary (Python convention, NOT one of the 11 standards)
observations.finding: Cross-module import of `_enumerate_sentinel_grant_files` from `lib.allowlist`. Leading underscore is Python convention for module-private symbols; reaching into a private function from another module is a mild code-smell.
observations.recommendation: (Soft) Either rename `_enumerate_sentinel_grant_files` to `enumerate_sentinel_grant_files` (drop the underscore, making it a public-API function) in hooks/lib/allowlist.py and update its single import site, OR keep it private and create a thin public wrapper like `has_sentinel_grant_for_task(task_id) -> bool` that internally calls the private enumerator. Not one of the 11 enumerated standards; logged for transparency.
observations.disposition_rationale: Not a Standard 1-11 violation. Python visibility convention is style-preference territory outside the inspector's scope.

observations.standard: Chinese chars on pre-existing lines outside diff-overlap (Standard 6 baseline)
observations.finding: Multiple pre-existing Chinese-character occurrences exist in command files and hook scripts. Per Standard 6's unconditional-scan exception (which this inspector ran), these are listed for transparency. Per AC-2.6 line-level rule, NONE of them are introduced_in_diff this cycle (they were present in HEAD baseline) and therefore are NOT cycle-blocking findings.
observations.recommendation: (Out of scope for this cycle.) A future cleanup cycle could review these pre-existing Chinese occurrences under Standard 6. Some are spec-reference quotes (e.g. commands/dev.md:357 《注入位置》 boilerplate is a verbatim spec quote with backticks) and may be exempt; others (commands/dev.md:1480-1482 planning paragraphs) may be legitimate violations. Inspector does NOT flag pre-existing items per AC-2.6 default-safe rule.
observations.disposition_rationale: Pre-existing; outside diff-overlap; out of cleanliness-of-this-diff scope per close.md AC-2.6.

verdict.summary: ONE CRITICAL violation introduced by cycle 20260519-211515 diff: commands/commit.md:130 contains a newly-added verbatim Chinese user-binding directive, which falls outside the Standard 6 docs/dev/ticket-*.md exemption scope. Two ADVISORY findings (Standard 3 hook python3 sites; Standard 9 ttl=300 magic number), both file-convention-consistent. One critical violation BLOCKS close-cleanliness unless (a) caller applies the proposed fix this cycle, or (b) the violation is explicitly accepted with documented rationale.

codex_consult.block_reason: PreToolUse:Bash hook (pretool-bash-safety.sh) blocked the codex CLI invocation. The bash hook detected literal substrings `kill -TERM`, `pkill -f`, and `killall` inside the codex prompt body (these were being PASSED to codex as part of explaining the context-notes section, which describes them as documented anti-patterns in qa.md/ba.md DO NOT clauses, NOT actual signal calls). The hook applies substring-matching to the entire bash command line including embedded quoted prompt content, with no awareness that the literals appear inside a codex prompt argument rather than as executable signals. Per CLAUDE.md 🛡️ Subagent Hook Discipline rule: 'When a hook rejects a command, the subagent MUST PAUSE immediately and report ... NOT circumvent via shell wrappers, intermediary scripts, hook-source recon, or hook-file edits.' Self-review (10 adversarial questions) substituted per inspector contract fallback.
codex_consult.verbatim_hook_output: [bash "$HOME/.claude/hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh"]: BLOCKED: kill with signals is forbidden — use graceful shutdown methods
codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex never executed; codex output file is empty (zero bytes). Inspector performed 10-question self-review (see codex_consult.self_review_questions[]) as the contract-mandated fallback.

self_review_questions.question: Is the Chinese phrase at commit.md:130 actually a Standard 6 violation at all (it has English translation + backtick wrapping)?
self_review_questions.verdict: Yes — the exemption clause is narrow ('docs/dev/ticket-*.md only'); commit.md is a command file, not a ticket file. The mitigating factors (English translation in surrounding prose, backtick wrapping) reduce harm but do not satisfy the literal exemption.
self_review_questions.action: Initial draft classified as ADVISORY; upgraded to CRITICAL on self-review based on strict literal reading of Standard 6 + narrow scope of exemption.

self_review_questions.question: Is the (1)/(2)/(3)/(4) numbering in commit.md Step 7 a Standard 4 violation?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — Standard 4 forbids decimal (1.1) or lettered (1a) numbering. Parenthesized integers are integer-sequence numbering within a single top-level step.

self_review_questions.question: Are the new Step 7 algorithm paragraphs in commit.md pseudocode (Standard 8 violation)?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — the algorithm describes regex strings, file paths, and decision criteria with concrete content; no '# Do X then Y' placeholder shell commands.

self_review_questions.question: Is the qa.md added bash code block actually an example or executable inline code (Standard 1)?
self_review_questions.verdict: Example — contains <spawn-command> placeholder and is explicitly framed as the canonical idempotent-cleanup pattern.

self_review_questions.question: Should ADVISORY 2 (python3 in hooks) be CRITICAL on literal reading of Standard 3?
self_review_questions.verdict: Borderline. Strict literal reading says yes; AC-2.6 file-convention-consistency rule (baseline established the pattern) + new pretool-bash-safety.sh sites DO use $PYTHON_BIN (showing intent to honor the standard where fresh) keep it ADVISORY.

self_review_questions.question: Should ADVISORY 3 (ttl=300) be CRITICAL?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — the value is documented by inline comment and the test fixtures don't bind on it. Refactor-opportunity, not blocker.

self_review_questions.question: Is hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py importing _enumerate_sentinel_grant_files (leading underscore) a Standard 5 or other violation?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — Python module-visibility convention is not one of the 11 enumerated standards. Logged as observation_only.

self_review_questions.question: Are there any newly-introduced inline executable code blocks in commands/dev.md or commands/close.md (Standard 1)?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — verified via diff that no `+```bash` or `+```python` blocks were added to those files.

self_review_questions.question: Did the cycle introduce any new merge-conflict markers (Standard 11)?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — grepped all 25 changed files; 0 hits for `^(<<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>) `.

self_review_questions.question: Are the pre-existing Chinese characters on lines NOT in the diff overlap (commands/dev.md:253,357,609 etc.) cycle-introduced violations?
self_review_questions.verdict: No — per AC-2.6 line-level rule, lines outside diff overlap with introduced_in_diff=false cannot be flagged as cycle-introduced. Logged in observations[] for transparency.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted; draft ADVISORY 1 (commit.md:130 Chinese phrase) UPGRADED to CRITICAL on adversarial self-review of strict-literal Standard 6 reading. ADVISORY 2 and ADVISORY 3 retained at advisory severity. One observation added (module-private import boundary). One observation added (pre-existing Chinese chars listed for transparency outside cycle scope).
